items.headline: Major commercial project approved on West San Carlos
items.summary: A $696,570 commercial/industrial development is underway at 1300 W San Carlos St, one of San José's key commercial corridors. This represents significant economic activity and potential new jobs in the area.

items.headline: Cupertino launches business license amnesty program for shopping centers
items.summary: The city is offering an amnesty program to help businesses at shopping centers get into compliance with licensing requirements. This could affect local business operations and shopping center tenants across Cupertino.

items.headline: New economic incentives proposed for retail and small businesses
items.summary: City Council discussed new incentive programs aimed at supporting retail, small businesses, and startups in Cupertino. These initiatives could impact the local business landscape and commercial development.

items.headline: Federal housing grant funding applications being reviewed
items.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission heard presentations from applicants seeking Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) funding on March 25. These federal dollars support local affordable housing and community development projects in Sunnyvale.

items.headline: San Antonio Road area plan study to explore land use and transportation changes
items.summary: The Planning Commission will study alternatives for how the San Antonio Road area should develop, including land use and transportation options. This study could shape future development and traffic patterns in that corridor.

items.headline: New retail vitality ordinance aims to revive struggling commercial districts
items.summary: The Commission is considering changes to zoning and building codes designed to implement retail policies from the Comprehensive Plan and support struggling retail areas. This could affect parking requirements, storefront design, and what types of businesses are allowed in commercial zones.

items.headline: Campbell updates beekeeping rules with new ordinance
items.summary: The City Council adopted an updated beekeeping ordinance that changes regulations for keeping bees in Campbell. The new rules affect residents interested in backyard beekeeping or urban agriculture.

items.headline: Three council members recuse themselves from transit development vote
items.summary: A public hearing on Senate Bill 79, which promotes transit-oriented housing development, proceeded with three council members recused due to property conflicts within the affected zone. The council invoked the 'rule of necessity' to ensure a quorum could vote on the state-mandated policy.
